name: SuperPawn
description_short: Tucson, AZ's SuperPawn at 3650 N 1st Ave #100 provides in-person pawn loans, buying, and selling of electronics, jewelry, and collectibles.
description_long:
SuperPawn in Tucson, AZ is located at 3650 N 1st Ave #100, a standalone store serving the north Tucson area. The store is open Monday through Saturday from 9AM to 7PM, and Sunday from 12PM to 5PM, making it convenient for Tucson shoppers and loan seekers.

At this Tucson location, you can secure pawn loans on electronics, jewelry, watches, musical instruments, and collectibles. The staff can also help you sell unwanted items or find quality used goods at competitive prices. Call 520-888-0001 to discuss your items or ask about current in-store options.

Whether you need quick cash, want to buy quality used items, or are selling something you no longer need, this SuperPawn store on N 1st Ave serves Tucson's pawn shop needs. Bring a valid government ID and your items ready for evaluation.
pros: 3,300+ retail locations across 29 U.S. states, DC, UK, and Latin America provide convenient local access
Non-recourse pawn loans mean borrowers cannot be pursued for deficiency—only collateral is at risk
No credit check required; serves credit-constrained consumers who don't qualify for traditional lending
Immediate cash on the spot for items pawned; fast funding without application delays
Diverse merchandise inventory (jewelry, electronics, tools, instruments) provides both lending and shopping options
Layaway service available with 10% down payment for consumers preferring installment-based buying
Buy gold and precious metals at established locations with on-the-spot cash payment
S&P 500-listed company with 22,000 employees and institutional oversight, indicating stability and regulatory compliance
cons: Pawn loans require surrendering personal property as collateral—items are forfeited if loan isn't repaid within the redemption period
Interest rates and terms not disclosed on website; rates vary by state and location, requiring in-store inquiry
Limited transparency on APR and fee structures; consumers must visit physical location to understand true cost of borrowing
Redemption periods and renewal policies not specified on website; unclear how long borrowers have to reclaim pledged items
Retail merchandise prices not publicly listed; inventory availability and pricing only visible at individual store locations
best_for: Consumers with immediate short-term cash needs who own valuable items and cannot obtain traditional loans
Cash-constrained individuals seeking to avoid credit checks and formal loan applications
People looking to buy secondhand merchandise at discounted prices from pawn store inventory
Borrowers who prefer non-recourse lending where collateral loss is the only consequence of non-repayment
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for consumers needing immediate cash who own valuables and lack access to traditional credit, as well as bargain hunters seeking secondhand merchandise. The primary caveat is that pawning requires surrendering personal property as collateral with the risk of permanent loss if the loan isn't repaid within the redemption period, and APR/fee transparency is limited without visiting a physical location.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property (jewelry, electronics, tools, instruments, sporting goods)
Retail sales of merchandise acquired through pawning transactions
Gold and precious metals buying with on-the-spot cash payment
Layaway payment plans with 10% down payment option
General merchandise buying—jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments
Store locator tool to find nearest FirstCash location among 3,300+ stores
Lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions through subsidiary AFF (15,000+ merchant partners)
Point-of-sale payment technology solutions for cash-constrained consumers
Online inventory browsing and store information
Pawn FAQs and educational resources on how pawn loans work
meta_description: SuperPawn at 3650 N 1st Ave, Tucson, AZ. Pawn loans and buying/selling jewelry, electronics & collectibles.
